originalAlarm.description: Value stored to '__m_has_modinfo_1618' is never read

sanitizedDescription: Value stored to 'm_has_modinfo' is never read
model:
(define-fun __need_malloc_and_calloc () Bool
  false)
(define-fun __USE_EXTERN_INLINES () Bool
  false)
(define-fun _STDLIB_H () Bool
  false)
reasoning: 5275b1e49f6.c L16: Dead store of `m_has_modinfo`. At L15-18, `if(r) m_has_modinfo=0; else m_has_modinfo=1;` — m_has_modinfo is assigned but never used after. New bug (no ERROR comment). The bug exists in all compilable configs (VERSION_CHECKING=T; VERSION_CHECKING=F → k_crcs undeclared at L25 → compile error). PC simplifies to !_STDLIB_H AND !__need_malloc_and_calloc — only library macros, no real feature flags. The `!_STDLIB_H` condition is restrictive (only satisfied before stdlib.h is fully included, not in real compilation). PC is over-constrained — misses the real compilation states (where _STDLIB_H=T) where the dead store also occurs.
